data.title: Amazon Pharmacy to offer Wegovy pill for as little as $25 a month
data.description:
Amazon (AMZN) is now offering Novo Nordisk’s Wegovy (NVO) in a pill form on its pharmacy platform, capitalizing on the popularity – and proliferation -- of weight-loss medications.

Customers can acquire the recently-approved Wegovy pill for as little as $25 per month with eligible insurance plans, or through a cash-pay option for $149. Amazon Pharmacy (AMZN) currently offers Wegovy, Ozempic (NVO), and Zepbound (LLY) pen injectors with a prescription.  

“By offering Wegovy through both insurance and a straightforward cash-pay option, we’re giving people more choice, greater transparency, and fewer barriers to care,” said Tanvi Patel, general manager of Amazon Pharmacy.

Healthcare providers can submit a prescription directly to the online Amazon Pharmacy (AMZN) or to an Amazon Pharmacy kiosk, enabling patients to receive their medication as soon as the same day. Customers can also access Amazon One Medical assistance for treatment recommendations, care management for weight loss, and clinical evaluation.

Amazon Pharmacy also collaborates with healthcare providers and digital health companies like Weight Watchers (WW), Wheel, and 9amHealth to expand access to weight loss medications and treatments.

The news is weighing on shares of Hims & Hers Health (HIMS), WW International (WW), GoodRx Holdings (GDRX), and LifeMD (LFMD), all of which offer comparable weight-loss medications, including Wegovy in pill form.

data.title: BigBear.ai partners with Kraft Group
data.description:
• BigBear.ai (BBAI) on Friday announced a new partnership with the Kraft Group.
  • As part of the partnership, BigBear.ai has entered into an agreement with the Kraft Group’s International Forest Products LLC (IFP) to strengthen supply chain transparency.
  • This work is focused on enhancing IFP’s visibility into global supply chains and identifying where AI-enabled insights can improve operational performance and commercial decision-making.
  • In addition, BigBear.ai (BBAI) has entered into a marketing partnership as an official sponsor of the New England Patriots.
  • BigBear.ai (BBAI) shares were up ~4%.

data.title: Glencore surges in Europe on talks with Rio Tinto to create world's largest miner
data.description:
Glencore (GLCNF) (GLNCY) +9.4% in London trading Friday to its highest in more than a year following news it is in talks with Rio Tinto (RIO) about a potential merger that would create the world's largest mining company, valued at more than $200B; Rio -4% in early U.S. trading.

Few details are known about the structure of a possible deal, but under U.K. takeover rules, Rio (RIO) has until February 5 to make a formal offer for Glencore (GLCNF) (GLNCY) or say it will not proceed.

Analysts say Rio (RIO) wants Glencore's (GLCNF) (GLNCY) potential growth in copper, whose price has jumped to record highs this week following a series of supply shocks at major mines, and exposure to the metal could lift the combined entity's shares.

Rio (RIO) needs more copper as "the market (rightly or wrongly) views iron ore as a commodity facing price decline," Berenberg analyst Richard Hatch said in a note, adding it was better to buy producing assets rather than wait to build new mines.

Hatch said a Glencore-Rio deal makes sense and followed successful mergers, such as the merger of Anglo American and Teck Resources, whose rationale was access to copper.

The combined company would overtake BHP (BHP), which has a market capitalization of more than $160B, and Hatch said BHP could move in with a rival bid.

Glencore's (GLCNF) (GLNCY) coal business has long been seen as a likely hurdle for potential buyers, and Rio Tinto (RIO) divested its coal assets in a decarbonization push, but Glencore's decision to retain its coal assets has generated substantial profits due to soaring prices in recent years.

Bloomberg reported that Rio (RIO) is open to retaining Glencore's (GLCNF) (GLNCY) coal business if merger talks are successful.

Glencore (GLCNF) (GLNCY) has been marketing itself for a sale for some time and is seen a willing seller, RBC analyst Ben Davis said in a note.

China, the dominant buyer of industrial metals and a stakeholder in Rio (RIO) via state-owned Chinalco, could raise antitrust hurdles, analysts also said.

ETFs: (CPER), (COPX), (JJCTF)

data.title: Clene announces registered direct offering of over $28M
data.description:
• Clene (CLNN) on Friday announced a registered direct offering priced above market under Nasdaq rules of over $28 million by new and existing investors.
  • On January 9, 2026, Clene entered into securities purchase agreements for the issuance and sale of: 928,333 shares of common stock and accompanying warrants at an offering price of $6.50 per unit for each investor, totaling $6.03 million of gross proceeds, which is expected to fund Clene into the third quarter of 2026;
  • Series A Warrants, which entitle each purchaser to purchase its pro rata share of the number of shares of common stock determined by dividing $6,684,000 by the applicable exercise price.
  • Each Series A Warrant will have an initial exercise price of $6.00 per share. All Series A Warrants will be exercisable immediately upon issuance.  
  • The exercise price of each Series A Warrant will increase to $7.00 per share if either: (a) the warrant is exercised prior to the company’s public announcement of the FDA’s posted PDUFA action date for the CNM-Au8 NDA, which is expected to occur in the first quarter of 2026; or (b) the volume-weighted average price of the Company’s common stock equals or exceeds $10.00 on the measurement date associated with the PDUFA Date Announcement. 
  • The potential gross proceeds from the exercise of the Series A Warrants totals approximately $6.7 million and is expected to fund the company through the end of 2026; and Series B Warrants, which entitle each purchaser to purchase its pro rata share of the number of shares of common stock determined by dividing $15,596,000 by the applicable exercise price.
  • Each Series B Warrant will have an initial exercise price of $6.00 per share. All Series B Warrants will be exercisable immediately upon issuance.  
  • The exercise price of each Series B Warrant is subject to increase based on the timing of exercise and the common stock’s volume-weighted average price in connection with the Company’s public announcement of its receipt of written FDA approval for the CNM-Au8 NDA in ALS.
  • The exercise price will increase to $12.50 per share if the Series B Warrant is exercised before the FDA Approval Announcement. If the Series B Warrant is exercised after the FDA Approval Announcement, it will increase to: (a) $10.00 per share, if the volume-weighted average price of Clene’s common stock on Nasdaq is equal to or greater than $20.00 on the measurement date associated with the FDA Approval Announcement or (b) $12.50 share, if the volume-weighted average price of Clene’s common stock on Nasdaq is equal to or greater than $25.00 on the measurement date. 
  • The potential gross proceeds from the exercise of the Series B Warrants totals about $15.6 million and is expected to fund the company’s commercialization efforts.
  • The Company may redeem or call the Series A Warrants and the Series B Warrants for cancellation if the closing price of the Company’s common stock exceeds the then-effective exercise price on or after the applicable measurement date. 
  • The gross proceeds from the offering are expected to be about $28M.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about January 12, 2026.
  • CLNN -6.7% premarket to $5.57.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Wall Street rises as key labor report comes largely in line
data.description:
The stock market inched higher on Friday as the key jobs report came largely as expected. 

S&P 500 (SP500) +0.2%, the Dow (DJI) +0.2%, and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) +0.1%.

December nonfarm payrolls growth came in roughly in line, while the unemployment rate dipped to 4.4%.

Treasury yields advanced across the curve. The U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) rose to 3.51%, up 1.7 basis points. The benchmark 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) climbed to 4.19%, adding 1.3 basis points. The 30-year Treasury yield (US30Y) ticked higher to 4.86%, up 1.6 basis points.

data.title: Metals Creek acquires 0.5% NSR on Tillex property from Blue Moon Metals
data.description:
• Metals Creek (MCREF) on Friday announced that it acquired a 0.5% net smelter return royalty on the Tillex property in Ontario from Blue Moon Metals.
  • The company had entered into a purchase agreement with Savant in November 2008 to acquire an 85% interest in the Tillex property, which the company had the right to purchase.
  • The company has now exercised that right, acquiring the 0.5% NSR in exchange for issuing 50,000 Metals Creek common shares to Blue Moon Metals.
  • Shares -26.33%.

data.title: Real estate tech stocks gap up amid Trump's housing relief push
data.description:
A number of housing-sensitive fintech stocks jumped in Friday premarket trading on the back of U.S. President Donald Trump's latest effort to bring down housing costs.

Offerpad Solutions (OPAD) shares surged 55% to $2.35 apiece, loanDepot (LDI) spiked 17%, Opendoor Technologies (OPEN) +11%, Blend Labs (BLND) +6% and LendingTree (TREE) +5.9% at press time.

The swings come after Trump on Thursday said he's instructing unnamed representatives to acquire $200B in mortgage bonds. His move is aimed at narrowing mortgage spreads and lowering borrowing costs, which could be a boon for loan origination volume and transaction demand for the aforementioned companies.

data.title: Bitmine Immersion Technologies appoints Young Kim as CFO, COO
data.description:
• Bitmine Immersion Technologies (BMNR) appointed Young Kim as chief financial officer, chief operating officer, and a member of its board of directors, effective immediately.
  • Kim brings over 20 years of investment experience, including senior portfolio management roles at Axiom Investors (2021–2025) and Columbia Threadneedle Investments (2011–2021).
  • Bitmine now owns 3.43% of the ETH token supply, two-thirds of the way to the 'Alchemy of 5%'.

data.title: Gorilla Technology updates share repurchase program, $9.6M capacity remaining
data.description:
• Gorilla Technology Group (GRRR) has repurchased ~$5M of shares, or about 354,000 shares, since increasing its buyback program to $20M in Q4 2025.
  • Total repurchases under the program now stand at ~$10.4M, leaving around $9.6M in remaining authorization.
  • Gorilla noted reduced share liquidity, attributing it to long-term shareholders holding shares.
  • The company will temporarily pause repurchases until after releasing its Full Year 2025 financial statements, with plans to resume if shares remain undervalued.
